description: Align Ponder indexer schema with frontend GraphQL query expectations using a generative approach. The generator automatically creates aggregate table definitions and handler code based on EVENT_TO_AGGREGATE_MAPPING configuration.

userStories.title: Define Assets aggregate table in Ponder schema
userStories.description: As a frontend developer querying the indexer, I need an 'assets' table so that queries like `assetss(limit: 100)` resolve correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Assets table defined in generateEntityTables() in scripts/generate-indexer.ts
Schema fields: id (bigint PK), name (text), asset_class (text), class_name (text), hash (hex), block_number (bigint), block_timestamp (bigint), transaction_hash (hex)
userStories.notes: Ponder pluralizes table names: 'assets' -> 'assetss'. Aggregate tables are generated automatically by the generator.

userStories.title: Define Orders aggregate table in Ponder schema
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need an 'orders' table so queries like `orderss(where: { buyer: $address })` work.
acceptanceCriteria: Orders table defined in generateEntityTables() in scripts/generate-indexer.ts

Schema fields: id (hex PK), clob_order_id (hex), ausys_order_id (hex), buyer (hex), seller (hex), token (hex), token_id (bigint), quantity (bigint), price (bigint), bounty (bigint), status (text), logistics_status (integer), block_number (bigint), block_timestamp (bigint), transaction_hash (hex)
Indexes on buyer, seller, token, token_id, status
userStories.notes: Status values: OPEN, FILLED, PARTIAL_FILL, CANCELLED, EXPIRED, COMPLETED

userStories.title: Define Journeys aggregate table in Ponder schema
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a 'journeys' table so queries like `journeyss(where: { status: 0 })` work for driver availability.
acceptanceCriteria: Journeys table defined in generateEntityTables() in scripts/generate-indexer.ts

Schema fields: id (hex PK), unified_order_id (hex), status (bigint), bounty (bigint), eta (bigint), start_lat (text), start_lng (text), end_lat (text), end_lng (text), block_number (bigint), block_timestamp (bigint), transaction_hash (hex)

userStories.title: Add EVENT_TO_AGGREGATE_MAPPING interface and generator logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a declarative configuration for mapping events to aggregate tables so the generator can produce handler code automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Define EVENT_TO_AGGREGATE_MAPPING constant with event -> aggregate mappings
Modify generateHandlerStub() to import aggregate tables
Generate aggregate inserts/updates in handler output
userStories.notes: The generator approach eliminates need for manual handler files for aggregates.

userStories.title: Map Orders events to aggregate updates
userStories.description: As a system, order events should automatically populate the orders aggregate table.
acceptanceCriteria: Add mappings for Diamond:OrderPlacedWithTokens -> orders insert
Add mappings for Diamond:RouterOrderPlaced -> orders insert
Add mappings for Diamond:CLOBOrderFilled, AusysOrderFilled -> orders update (status)
Add mappings for CLOBOrderCancelled, MatchingOrderCancelled, RouterOrderCancelled, OrderExpired -> orders update (status)
Verify generated clob.generated.ts includes aggregate logic
userStories.notes: Status logic: FILL_CHECK -> FILLED if remainingAmount=0, else PARTIAL_FILL

userStories.title: Map Journeys events to aggregate updates
userStories.description: As a system, logistics events should automatically populate the journeys aggregate table.
acceptanceCriteria: Add mapping for Diamond:LogisticsOrderCreated -> journeys insert
Add mapping for Diamond:JourneyStatusUpdated -> journeys update (status)
Verify generated handlers include journey aggregate updates
userStories.notes: journeyId comes from event args, unifiedOrderId from logisticsOrderCreated

userStories.title: Map Assets events to aggregate updates
userStories.description: As a system, AuraAsset events should automatically populate the assets aggregate table.
acceptanceCriteria: Add mapping for AuraAsset:MintedAsset -> assets insert
AuraAsset artifact must exist in artifacts/contracts/
Verify generated aura-asset.generated.ts includes asset aggregate updates
userStories.notes: Requires AuraAsset.sol to be compiled. Currently artifact not present.

userStories.title: Create smoke test for Assets GraphQL query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated verification that the assets query works against the running indexer.
acceptanceCriteria: Test queries `assetss(limit: 10)` against INDEXER_URL env var
Verify response has shape: { assetss: { items: [...] } }
userStories.notes: Use graphql-request or fetch. INDEXER_URL defaults to http://localhost:42069

userStories.title: Create smoke test for Orders GraphQL query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated verification that orders queries work including buyer/seller filters.
userStories.notes: Status values in schema are text strings, not integers

userStories.title: Create smoke test for Journeys GraphQL query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated verification that journeys queries work including status filters.
userStories.notes: Status is BigInt, filter should use numeric value

userStories.title: Verify frontend GraphQL queries match Ponder schema
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need confidence that repository queries align with the indexer schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Document any field name mismatches (snake_case vs camelCase)
userStories.notes: Ponder schema uses snake_case. Frontend may need updates.

userStories.title: Run full smoke test suite and document results
userStories.description: As a developer completing this feature, I need all smoke tests passing and results documented.
acceptanceCriteria: Start local indexer: `cd indexer && npm run dev`
Run all smoke tests: `cd indexer && npm test -- --grep smoke`
